slides.title: Sitting still for hours has *its own cost*.
slides.sub: One workout can't undo a whole day in the chair. The fix is small, frequent movement.

slides.title: Long sitting is its own risk
slides.body: Hours of stillness *may quietly affect* your blood sugar and circulation, even when you exercise later that day.

slides.title: Frequent breaks beat one fix
slides.body: Spreading movement through the day matters more than a single session. The goal is to interrupt the sitting often.

slides.title: A two minute walk
slides.body: Stand and move for *just two minutes* roughly every hour. A lap of the room or kitchen is plenty.

slides.title: Tie it to a cue
slides.body: Move when a meeting ends, the kettle boils, or your hourly reminder pings. Let everyday moments prompt you.

slides.title: Motion through the day
slides.body: You don't need a gym to break up sitting. Small, regular movement *can support* a healthier, longer life.

slides.title: Keep your day *gently moving*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your steps and movement and turns every walk break into one simple daily score you can build on.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Sitting still for hours carries its own cost, and one workout can't fully undo it. 🌿

The real fix is small and frequent: a two minute walk every hour, tied to a cue you already have. Movement through the day beats a single session. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns every walk break into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: Why Breaking Up Sitting Matters More Than One Workout
